LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. PLAINTIFF ANTHONY CURATOLO'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT'S COMPLIANCE WITH DOCUMENTS IS DENIED. THOUGH TITLED A MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE, THIS MATTER IS A MOTION TO COMPEL A FURTHER RESPONSE. THEREFORE, THE MOTION IS UNTIMELY PER CCP 2031.310. AFTER HEARING, THE COURT SIGNED THE PROPOSED ORDER SENT BY EMAIL. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. Off Calendar Jul-24-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: YOSEF PERETZ, ESQ., 415.732.3777 X102, FOR PLAINTIFF ANTHONY CURATOLO.
PETER BERTRAND, ESQ., 415.227.0900, FOR DEFENDANT JAN CASEY.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling with modification: PLAINTIFF ANTHONY CURATOLO's Motion To Strike Or, In The Alternative, Tax Costs is denied in its entirety. Defendant's costs memo is timely under CRC 3.1700 and Fries v. Rite Aid Corp. (2009) 173 Cal.App.4th 182. The deposition costs were reasonable and defendant properly substantiates them. (Bertrand dec., Ex. 4). The Court further notes that plaintiff failed to provide a copy of the costs memo or object to the costs in compliance with CRC 3.1700(b)(2). The court denies plaintiff's attempt to tax $390 because that issue was raised for the first time in the reply brief. Even if the court were to consider the merits of the $390, the court finds they are allowable under CCP section 1033.5. Order signed in open court. Judgment signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Off Calendar Nov-09-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:
DEFENDANT JAN CASEY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - The demurrer was heard on November 8, 2012, per the amended notice. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 = (302/MJM)

AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-08-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. YOSEF PERETZ OF PERETZ & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 732-3777 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. KATE KIMBERLIN OF LONG & LEVIT LLP, TEL. NO. 438-4408 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT JANE GRONER;
ATTY. PETER BERTRAND OF BUCHALTER NEMER LAW CORPORATION, TEL. NO. 227-0900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT JAN CASEY BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JAN CASEY'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - The Court sustains the demurrer to the conversion cause of action. The statute of limitations for that claim is three years under CCP 338. Plaintiff relies on the discovery rule to avoid the statute of limitations. In order to invoke the discovery rule, the plaintiff must specifically plead facts which show (1) the time and manner of discovery and (2) the inability to have made earlier discovery despite reasonable diligence. See Saliter v. Pierce Brothers Mortuaries (1978) 81 Cal.App.3d 292, 297. Here, paragraph 20 alleges facts regarding the time and manner of discovery. Plaintiff, however, does not allege any facts regarding why the discovery could not have occurred earlier. Plaintiff has 20 days leave to amend to plead facts to support the second prong of the discovery rule. The Court rejects defendant's remaining arguments on the conversion cause of action. The demurrer to the unjust enrichment cause of action is overruled. The Court strikes the claim for punitive damages with leave to amend because plaintiff's tort claim against Jan Casey is possibly time-barred. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312) Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-08-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. YOSEF PERETZ OF PERETZ & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 732-3777 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. KATE KIMBERLIN OF LONG & LEVIT LLP, TEL. NO. 438-4408 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT JANE GRONER;
ATTY. PETER BERTRAND OF BUCHALTER NEMER LAW CORPORATION, TEL. NO. 227-0900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT JAN CASEY BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JANE GRONER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - The demurrer to breach of contract, breach of implied covenant and specific performance causes of action are overruled. Plaintiff properly pleads the existence and terms of the contract according to its legal intendment and effect. The Court sustains the demurrer to the conversion and fraud causes of action. The statute of limitations for those claims is three years under CCP 338. Plaintiff relies on the discovery rule to avoid the statute of limitations. In order to invoke the discovery rule, the plaintiff must specifically plead facts which show (1) the time and manner of discovery and (2) the inability to have made earlier discovery despite reasonable diligence. See Saliter v. Pierce Brothers Mortuaries (1978) 81 Cal.App.3d 292, 297. Here, paragraph 20 alleges facts regarding the time and manner of discovery. Plaintiff, however, does not allege any facts regarding why the discovery could not have occurred earlier. Plaintiff has 20 days leave to amend to plead facts to support the second prong of the discovery rule. The Court rejects defendant's remaining arguments on the fraud and conversion causes of action. The demurrer to the accounting cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff does not need to allege a fiduciary relationship to support that cause of action and the statute of limitations is 4 years. Estate of Peebles, (1972) 27 Cal.App.3d 163, 166. The demurrer to the unjust enrichment cause of action is overruled. The Court strikes the claim for punitive damages with leave to amend because plaintiff's tort claims are possibly time-barred.(Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312) Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/MJM
